title: Energy intensity vs. GDP per capita
subtitle: Energy intensity represents [primary energy](#dod:primaryenergy) consumption, using the [substitution method](#dod:substitutionmethod), per unit of gross domestic product (GDP). GDP is adjusted for inflation and differences in living costs between countries.
caption:
Energy intensity vs. GDP per capita, 2022

Energy intensity represents primary energy consumption, using the substitution method, per unit of gross domestic product (GDP). GDP is adjusted for inflation and differences in living costs between countries.

This scatter plot maps the relationship between economic output and energy efficiency for nations in 2022, plotting energy intensity in kilowatt-hours per dollar against GDP per capita on a logarithmic scale. The data points, color-coded by continent, show a general negative relationship where higher income levels coincide with lower energy intensity, though the spread is wide. At the upper extreme of energy intensity, Turkmenistan, Bahrain, and North Korea occupy the top of the chart, consuming over 4 kWh per dollar of GDP. Venezuela and Zimbabwe also appear in the high-intensity band between 2 and 4 kWh. In the middle of the distribution, countries like Russia, Iran, and Canada cluster between 2,000 and 20,000 dollars of GDP per capita with intensity levels ranging from 2 to 2.5 kWh. The lower right section of the chart contains high-income economies with high efficiency; Norway, Denmark, and Switzerland sit below 1.5 kWh per dollar while exceeding 50,000 dollars in GDP per capita. Qatar stands as an outlier in the far right, combining very high GDP per capita with moderate energy intensity around 1.5 kWh. At the lower left, nations such as Burundi, Liberia, and Chad show low GDP per capita below 2,000 dollars and low energy intensity under 1 kWh. The visual pattern shows that while wealthier nations generally use less energy per unit of economic output, specific resource-rich or industrial economies maintain high energy intensity regardless of their GDP per capita level.
